perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall provide the Confidential Information only to those of its employees and directors (and any agents, representatives, consultants, or financial advisors that have been approved in advance by the Disclosing Party) that have a bona fide need to know for the Purpose, and in the case of AST to any of its current or prospective member companies, in each case who are bound by a written agreement including confidentiality and non-use terms 2 sd-409605 v4 and conditions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Except for the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall provide the Confidential Information only to those of its employees and directors that have a bona fide need to know for the Purpose. The Receiving Party may also disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without prior approval from the Disclosing Party. Except for the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction regarding disclosure to third parties. The original text required advance approval for disclosing information to agents, representatives, consultants, or financial advisors, and strictly prohibited disclosure to other third parties, while the modified text permits disclosure to 'any third party' without approval. This creates in-text contradiction regarding the recipient's obligations, leading to disputes over whether or not certain disclosures are permitted. The repetition of 'Except for the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party.' emphasizes the in-text contradiction and increases the potential for confusion and dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall provide the Confidential Information only to those of its employees and directors that have a bona fide need to know for the Purpose. The Receiving Party may also disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without prior approval from the Disclosing Party. Except for the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party. The Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose. No rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are to be implied from this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text strictly limited disclosure of Confidential Information to specific individuals and prohibited disclosure to third parties without prior approval. The modified text explicitly permits disclosure to 'any third party' without approval. The inclusion of 'Except for the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party' in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text emphasizes the in-text contradiction. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the recipient's obligations, leading to disputes over whether or not certain disclosures are permitted, and creating an inconsistency as defined in the perturbation definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed, except that the Receiving Party may retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information, to be used only in case of a dispute concerning this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed. The Receiving Party is not allowed to retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a contradiction by removing the clause allowing the Receiving Party to retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information. The original text permitted retaining an archival copy for dispute resolution, while the modified text explicitly prohibits this. This creates uncertainty about the Receiving Party's obligations regarding archival copies and whether they are allowed to keep them, potentially leading to disputes and creating in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party and will not be copied or reproduced without the express written permission of the Disclosing Party, except for copies that are necessary in order to fulfill the Purpose. Within ten (10) business days of a written demand by a Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall either return (to the Disclosing Party) or destroy all Confidential Information, together with any copies, accompanied by a letter executed by an authorized representative of the Receiving Party to the effect that all such material has been returned or destroyed. The Receiving Party is not allowed to retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information. The return of any Confidential Information will not relieve the Receiving Party of its obligation to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information for the period provided for in Section 1 above. Section 6 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states 'The Receiving Party is not allowed to retain an archival copy of the Confidential Information.' while the original text allowed the Receiving Party to retain an archival copy for dispute resolution. This creates a direct contradiction as the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this scenario, now contains a clause that conflicts with the original intent of allowing archival copies, satisfying the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the entire understanding and agreement of the Parties with respect to the disclosure of the Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, representations and discussions concerning the subject matter hereof, whether oral or written.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the core understanding of the Parties with respect to the disclosure of the Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, but does not supersede any prior oral agreements, understandings, representations and discussions concerning the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The modification contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause. The original text explicitly states that the agreement supersedes all prior agreements, whether oral or written. The changed text indicates that prior oral agreements are still valid, creating uncertainty and in-text contradiction about which agreements are enforceable. This directly impacts the interpretation and enforcement of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the core understanding of the Parties with respect to the disclosure of the Confidential Information by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, but does not supersede any prior oral agreements, understandings, representations and discussions concerning the subject matter hereof. This Agreement may not be altered or amended except by a written instrument executed by both parties. This Agreement will be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Delaware, without regard to its principles of conflicts of law. The Parties agree that a breach of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party for which money damages alone could be an inadequate remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text has an 'entire agreement' clause, superseding all prior agreements. The changed text explicitly states that prior oral agreements are still valid, creating a direct contradiction. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, thus the contradiction is within the same text. This aligns with the given explanation of the perturbation and the definition of inconsistencies in text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party. Nothing herein shall obligate either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party. Each party is obligated to fully disclose all Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text imposes an obligation to disclose all confidential information, directly contradicting the original clause that states 'Nothing herein shall obligate either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information'. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about whether a party is required to disclose information or has the right to refuse, making the contract ambiguous and difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party shall have the right to refuse to accept any Confidential Information, or other information, tendered by the other Party. Each party is obligated to fully disclose all Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party. Each Party hereto shall not be obligated to enter into any further agreement with the other except as such party in its sole judgment may deem advisable. Neither Party shall be obligated to compensate the other for exchanging any information under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Nothing herein shall obligate either Party to disclose any particular Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party' with 'Each party is obligated to fully disclose all Confidential Information, or other information, to the other Party.' This introduces a direct and irreconcilable contradiction. The original text explicitly states no obligation to disclose, while the changed text mandates full disclosure. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text further emphasizing the contradiction. This makes the agreement ambiguous and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose, or any other commercially reasonable use.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the restriction on use. The original text strictly limited the use of Confidential Information to 'the Purpose'. The modified text adds 'or any other commercially reasonable use', creating uncertainty about the permissible uses of the information. This significantly broadens the scope of permissible use and contradicts the intended restriction outlined in the original agreement, leading to potential disputes about what constitutes a 'commercially reasonable use'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party shall provide the Confidential Information only to those of its employees and directors that have a bona fide need to know for the Purpose. The Receiving Party may also disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without prior approval from the Disclosing Party. Except for the foregoing, the Receiving Party shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party. The Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose, or any other commercially reasonable use. No rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are to be implied from this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the Receiving Party could only use Confidential Information for 'the Purpose.' The changed text broadens this by adding 'or any other commercially reasonable use.' The contradicted text states 'The Receiving Party shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose, or any other commercially reasonable use.' The change introduces an inconsistency, which falls under the definition 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: inconsistencies also lead to in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties,' making the 'Purpose' limitation less strict.
